framework.name: Speed vs Security Trade-off in Coordination
framework.description: Coordination layers face a fundamental dilemma: Fort Knox security with slow finality (12+ minutes) vs lightning speed with network lock-in. The innovation is achieving near-instant finality (6 seconds) while borrowing security from a slower but more secure base layer.
framework.raw_quote: Do you want the Fort Knox level security you get with Ethereum, but have to wait over 12 minutes for your transaction to be truly 100% final? Or do you want the lightning speed of an L2, but risk getting stuck on that one specific network?
